title: Request to Amend Valid Temporary Resident Documents, or Information Contained in the Confirmation of Permanent Residence or Protected Person Document (IMM 1436) - Canada.ca
content:
## Request to Amend Valid Temporary Resident Documents, or Information Contained in the Confirmation of Permanent Residence or Protected Person Document (IMM 1436)
Request to Amend Valid Temporary Resident Documents, or Information Contained in the Confirmation of Permanent Residence or Protected Person Document [IMM 1436] (PDF, 332 MB)

### How to download, save and open this form
- Use your computer. The form may not open on tablets or mobile phones .
- Save the form on your computer in a place you can remember. For most Internet browsers, clicking on the link above will automatically download the form. If the form doesn’t automatically download, right-click on the link and select “Save as.”
- For most Internet browsers, clicking on the link above will automatically download the form.
- If the form doesn’t automatically download, right-click on the link and select “Save as.”
- After you download the form, open it using Adobe Acrobat Reader.

#### Open this form in Acrobat Reader
- Open Acrobat Reader. You need Acrobat Reader version 10 or higher to open our forms. Get the latest version of Acrobat Reader .
- You need Acrobat Reader version 10 or higher to open our forms.
- Get the latest version of Acrobat Reader .
- Select “ File ” from the top menu.
- Find the location where you saved the form, click on the file, and click “ Open. ”
Read the step by step instructions on how to complete the form.

### Request to Amend Valid Temporary Resident Documents, or Information Contained in the Confirmation of Permanent Residence or Protected Person Document
Complete Parts A, B, C , and the Declaration and Signature section of the Request to Amend Valid Temporary Resident Documents, or Information Contained in the Confirmation of Permanent Residence or Protected Person Document [IMM 1436] application form, unless otherwise indicated. If your application is not complete it will be returned to you. The information below is provided to help you complete the form.
Important information
It is a serious offence to give false or misleading information on these forms. The information you provide on your application is subject to verification.
In order to help you fill out the application form, read and follow the steps below.

### Part A – Personal details contained on your valid temporary resident document, Confirmation of Permanent Residence or protected person document
Indicate your Client Identification number (client ID) or Unique Client Identifier (UCI), if known. Otherwise leave it blank.
Note: If you have not dealt with IRCC since 1973, you will not have a UCI or a Client ID.
Indicate your surname(s) (family name) as it appears on the immigration document that you are seeking to amend.
Indicate your given name(s) as it appears on the immigration document that you are seeking to amend.
Indicate all other surnames (family names) you have ever used, including different spellings and explain what they are. For example: ‘Smith’ (birth name/maiden name), ‘Leroux’ (former married name).
Indicate all other given name(s) you have ever used, including different spellings and nicknames.
Check the box to indicate your gender/sex ( F -Female, M -Male or X -Another gender) as it appears on your immigration document that you are seeking to amend. If your document does not display your gender/sex, indicate your gender/sex as it appears on your passport.
Indicate your place of birth as it appears on your immigration document that you are seeking to amend. Include the city and jurisdiction (province/territory/state). If your document does not display your place of birth, indicate your place of birth as it appears on your passport.
Indicate your country of birth as it appears on your immigration document that you are seeking to amend.
Indicate your citizenship as it appears on your immigration document that you are seeking to amend. To be a citizen of a country means that you were either born in that country (in most cases) or you were granted citizenship in that country. If you have dual citizenship, select the country that issued the passport or travel document that you used to enter Canada/on your original application.
Indicate the country that issued the passport/travel document you used upon entry to Canada/on your original application.
If you do not have a copy of this passport/travel document, indicate the country that issued your current passport/travel document.
Indicate the passport/travel document number of the document you used upon entry to Canada/on your original application.
If you do not have a copy of this passport/travel document, indicate your current passport/travel document number.
Check the box to indicate if this passport/travel document number was the one used at the time of entry/on your original application or if it is your current passport/travel document number.
Indicate the date of issue of the passport/travel document you used in box 12 in the following format: YYYY-MM-DD (Year-Month-Day).
Indicate your marital status as it appears on your document to be amended. Not all documents show your marital status. If there is no marital status on your document, check the box that applies to your marital status when you entered Canada or applied for your original document:
- Married: This means that you and your spouse had a ceremony that legally binds you to each other. Your marriage must be legally recognized in the country where it was performed, and in Canada.
- Widowed: This means that your spouse has died and you have not re-married or entered into a new common-law relationship.
- Separated: this means that you are married but officially no longer living with your spouse.
- Divorced: This means that you are officially separated and legally ended your marriage.
- Common-law partner: this means that you have lived with your partner in a common-law relationship for a minimum of one year.
Indicate your current marital status, if it has changed since you entered Canada or since you applied for your original document.
Indicate the language of correspondence (English or French) you wish to use.
Indicate your current mailing address, including:
- Post Office (P.O.) Box number (if applicable)
- Apartment or unit number
- District (if applicable)
- Jurisdiction (Province/Territory/State)
If the country in which you reside does not use any of these values, please ensure a complete address for the country in which you reside is included.
Note: If you are requesting an amendment to a valid temporary resident document, a Canadian mailing address must be provided.
Check the box to indicate if your current residential address (where you live) is the same or different than your mailing address.
Indicate your current residential address (where you live) if it is different from your mailing address, including:
- Apartment or unit number
- District (if applicable)
- Jurisdiction (Province/Territory/State)
If the country in which you live does not use any of these values, please ensure a complete address for the country in which you reside is included.
Check the box to indicate whether your telephone number is from Canada and the United States (US), or another country.
In the fields below, indicate your telephone number, including:
- Type - Indicate if your telephone number belongs to your residence, cell or business.
- Indicate the country code.
- No. - Indicate your telephone number including the area code.
- Ext. - Indicate the extension, if applicable
If you have an alternative phone number, check the box to indicate whether your alternate telephone number is from Canada and the United States (US), or another country.
In the fields below, indicate your alternate telephone number, including:
- Type - Indicate if your telephone number belongs to your residence, cell or business.
- Indicate the country code.
- No. - Indicate your alternate phone number including the area code.
- Ext. - Indicate the extension, if applicable.
Clearly indicate your e-mail address using this format (if applicable): name@provider.net
Note : By indicating your e-mail address, you are hereby authorizing transmission of correspondence including file and personal information to be sent to you via e-mail to the address provided. If no email address is provided, IRCC will communicate with you by regular mail.
Ensure your e-mail address will be valid for the entire processing period.
Indicate the date you originally entered Canada, as it appears on your immigration document that you are seeking to amend, in the following format: YYYY-MM-DD (Year-Month-Day). Not all documents show the date you originally entered. If there is no date of original entry on your document, enter the date you first entered Canada, if known.
Indicate the date permanent residence or protected person status was granted, as it appears on your immigration document that you are seeking to amend, or, indicate the date your temporary residence document was issued. Enter in the following format: YYYY-MM-DD (Year-Month-Day).
Note: A protected person includes individuals found to be Convention refugees or persons in need of protection.

### Part B – information to be amended
Check the appropriate box to indicate what type of document needs a correction.
Note : If you are requesting an amendment to a temporary resident document, the document must still be valid (not expired).
For each valid temporary resident document that you are requesting a correction, indicate the document number, or if unknown, the document issue date and document expiry date of the requested document.
Indicate your surname(s) (family name) as it should appear on your immigration document.
If you are not asking for this information to be changed, leave this section blank.
Indicate your given name(s) as it should appear on your immigration document.
If you are not asking for this information to be changed, leave this section blank.
Indicate your date of birth as it should appear on your immigration document in the following format: YYYY-MM-DD (Year-Month-Day).
If you are not asking for this information to be changed, leave this section blank.
Indicate your place of birth (city/town and jurisdiction [state/province/territory]) as it should appear on your immigration document.
If you are not asking for this information to be changed, leave this section blank.
Indicate your country of birth as it should appear on your immigration document.
If you are not asking for this information to be changed, leave this section blank.
Indicate which country you are a citizen of as it should appear on your immigration document.
If you are not asking for this information to be changed, leave this section blank.
Indicate your gender/sex ( F -Female, M -Male or X -Another gender) as it should appear on your immigration document. Your record will only be amended free of charge if your gender/sex was recorded incorrectly at the time of entry to Canada/when you originally applied and you can provide proof that an error was made by an immigration official. If the information was not recorded in error and you want to change your gender, make sure you include all additional required documents to support your application, or it may be refused.
If you are not asking for this information to be changed, leave this section blank.
Indicate your marital status as it should appear on your immigration document. Your record will only be amended if your marriage status was recorded incorrectly at the time of entry to Canada/when you originally applied and you can provide proof that an error was made by an immigration official. If your marital status has changed after you entered Canada, it will not be amended.
If you are not asking for this information to be changed, leave this section blank.
Indicate the date of original entry to Canada as it should appear on your immigration document in the following format: YYYY-MM-DD (Year-Month-Day).
If you are not asking for this information to be changed, leave this section blank.
Indicate the date permanent residence was granted (if you are a permanent resident) or the date you became a protected person, as it should appear on your immigration document. If you are a temporary resident, indicate the date that should appear on your document (ie. date of issue). Enter the date in the following format: YYYY-MM-DD (Year-Month-Day).
If you are not asking for this information to be changed, leave this section blank.
Indicate the change to be made if it is not described in questions 1 to 11 above.
If you are not asking for any additional information to be changed, leave this section blank.
Note that only information described in this guide can be amended. Refer to the following sections:
- What information may be corrected on the CoPR, protected person document or other eligible document? and What information may be corrected on valid temporary resident documents?
What information may be corrected on the CoPR, protected person document or other eligible document?
What information may be corrected on valid temporary resident documents?
Check the appropriate box to indicate if, since your admission to Canada as a permanent resident, you have been convicted of a crime or offence in Canada or elsewhere. If you check “ yes ”, attach any copies of your pertinent court documents.
If you are a temporary resident or Canadian citizen , leave this section blank.
Provide an explanation of the reasons why you are requesting the amendment(s). You may attach a separate piece of paper if more space is required. Outline the error that was made, what information was provided in your application, and any appropriate details as to why a change is required.

### Declaration and Signature
Important information:
Read all of the statements in all sections carefully and:
- Sign and date in the space provided.
- If you are under 18 years of age, your parent or legal guardian must also sign and date the application.
By signing, you certify that you fully understand the questions asked, and that the information you have provided is complete, truthful, and correct. If you do not sign and date the application, it will be returned to you.

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
